content.claim: Hungary's birth rate has fallen sharply (fertility rate 1.6 in 2021 to 1.3 by 2026, with Q1 2026 at 1.29), resulting in ~20% decline in births over four years and 17,000 fewer one-year-olds than four-year-olds, with regional variance of 20%+ birth declines in several counties
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Fertility projections depend on behavioral assumptions that may shift with policy changes
Regional variance reported qualitatively ('several counties'); exact county-by-county figures not provided
Does not establish causal link between overall cohort decline and selective-track viability
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
